title: Task description
excerpt:
Hello everyone, . Does anyone know how can i add a description page for each task? 
I would prefer not from the update windows…

posts.author: Ofir_Haim (Ofir)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello everyone, . Does anyone know how can i add a description page for each task?
I would prefer not from the update windows…

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Ofir_Haim

Welcome to the community! I would recommend adding a long text column to the task. It does not use up space on your board until you click on it. The text inside this column pops up when you hover over it. The only drawback is that there is not any formatting (bold, underline etc) poddibility.

posts.author: Nicky (Nicky)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature is still needed. The text box is not sufficient. I want to click on the Task Name to pop up a screen with a dynamic text field including text formatting and ability to add screenshots and attachments - similar to a JIRA ticket description. There needs to be a space to define the task in detail and I haven’t found any other features that are sufficient for this purpose.

posts.author: Monty123 (Nit)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, i would second that. This is really a deal beaker feature for me. Not being able to express the task description well on each task with rich formatting is really surprising for such a mature app.

Can the be prioritised?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
I thought it might be helpful to outline the current options available in this area in base monday.

Long Text column type - as mentioned by Bas, is pure text, expanded when user hovers over it.
Updates - as alluded to by @Ofir_Haim, allows rich text style editing, user can pin note to the top of the page.
monday workdocs - powerful newly released functionality that allows complex document editing including embedding board views, widgets, and more. Can be attached to board items via the Files column.
CommuniHub Notes - Allows rich text style notes to be entered that are only viewable by board members.
** Info Box Notes ** - Allows rich text style notes to be entered on an item. (Are being discontinued according to monday.)

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all!

As @JCorrell explained, we do have a number of options that could work here.

I would recommend using the updates section as it allows for formatting and pinning to the top of the item.
You can create an automation to automatically add the update to the item when a new item is created, which would remove any need to manually add the update to each task.

Some users use this function to create templates that a user can then copy and fill out in an additional update to make sure all the required information is included. You could combine this with a notification to remind the user to read the update / fill out the necessary information.

I can definitely appreciate how the ability to create a pop up with screenshots and more advanced formatting would be a useful addition to the platform for yourself, and I’m sure for other teams too, so I would highly recommend opening a thread in our Feature Request section.
Here other users who would also like this added to the platform will be able to upvote the feature!
You can find this here: Submit an Idea - monday Community Forum

I hope this helps! Remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Best,
Dani

posts.author: Idoger (Ido)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi
We are using updates for task descriptions, as you recommended, but a big drawback for us is that only the person who created the update can edit it. This can be quite annoying when used for detailed description which are then used by the developers. Is there any way to have multiple users editing the same update?

posts.author: philippevk (Philippe VK)
posts.cookedHtml:
The solutions listed all have drawbacks for us. From best to worst:

  • info box note => discontinued (our account has it for now, but for how long?)
  • file column + doc => writing doc is very slow & awkward
  • long text column => bad edit UI, not great display
  • updates => only author can edit
  • CommuniHub notes => unsure, didn’t find what it is

File column + doc is very close to what we need, but the “ordered lines” paradigm doesn’t work well for task descriptions. Simple text editor (plain or rich) would work better for us.

posts.author: DMarken (Danny)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed! It’s quite irritating. It seems every project management tool has that one stupidly simple thing that they just cant to.

posts.author: guiboni (Guilherme)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m really shocked monday doesn’t have this basic feature.

posts.author: raiman (Dave Raiman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Still no option for a detailed description now 3 years later?

Closest match to a rich editor to provide detail of a task is pinning an update. Big drawback is that it still is only editable by the originator of the update and not a collaborative component of the task.

posts.author: Trey_Sparks (Trey Sparks)
posts.cookedHtml:
Adding another ping here. There’s a lot that Monday.com can do- but we used descriptions as a core element of how we communicate and work as a team- a central place for links, data, bullets, etc. We all use it to clarify the details of what needs to be done and to add rich context to what has been accomplished.

The other options provided are clunky and require extra steps (and clicks!!) for the team. If you can make this a priority I know our team would appreciate it.

posts.author: nerves76 (Chris Bolton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Just adding to the chorus here. Just started using Monday and was like “Where the hell is the task description?”

Seems like a simple feature and a big omission. I tried the workarounds and they are not ideal.

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml: The Monday Dev product actually does have a task description feature.

posts.author: nerves76 (Chris Bolton)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah, I saw that. It’s greyed out for me. I don’t understand why it would be just for dev. Don’t all tasks need descriptions?

posts.author: RossTrev (Ross.brown@vml.com)
posts.cookedHtml: I see a new “Item Description” View for the “Updates” window. This is very helpful to leverage when creating a Project Template, but it is very likely that the majority of the Item Descriptions will evolve as processes change and we receive feedback from users. I am opting to use a monday Doc that can be a central source of truth and can be referenced by Team Members.

posts.author: LukeHGA (Luke Hanson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Seems that i’m facing the same challenges as a number of users!

Fundamental basics of Project Management and delivery is to be clear with what you are trying to achieve. As such, a formattable text field, which can be collaborated on to articulate requirements is an absolute must have.

The work arounds are just not appropriate and seem excessive in comparison to Monday.com creating this capability.

Without such a field, the integration to Jira is rendered somewhat useless - as you lose visibility of what you’re trying to achieve, barring the title of the Work Item.
